transcript.clean_text: 175 riders set out from Paris but only one could take the victory in the Ruby Valley Durham a contest decided by 257 gruelling kilometres and 29 cobbled sections notably absent however for the 15th consecutive year was the rain once again not playing a part in proceedings as usual a permanent breakaway group formed from the beginning as nine hopeful riders looked for an early lead among them the Spaniard Marc select recent winner of pari nice with the wind at their backs the riders covered ground quickly in the first part of the race but after several Falls it was a smaller peloton which later entered at the trench of Ehrenburg cobbled section number 19 and first arbitrator of pari Roubaix Mike Tennyson and Phillip Gilbert took the chance to put some distance ahead of the favourites but the German Nils Philippe soon made this duo a trio judging them to be too dangerous Peter Sagan and the Bora hansgrohe formation put a swift end to the trios ambitions several riders made attempts to attack in a rush of movement but it was the world champion who drove his effort home 53 kilometers from the finish Peter Sagan cut himself loose from the pack of favorites and set upon the three survivors of the morning's breakaway on entering the cobbled section at Mons on Pavel the Slovak clocked a 44 second lead ahead of the favorites and among the six strongest to break away from the peloton were Taylor Finney Jasper's truven Zeppelin mark and Greg van a Vermonter Sagan was impressive as he led the race the world champion was closely followed by jolly valets and Sylvan Delia as he increased his lead to one minute 30 seconds with 20 kilometers to go at the car for the lob it was only Delia the swiss who was able to keep up with Sagan's unstoppable rhythm while the following group finally began to close the gap under the drive of niki terpstra it wasn't to be enough however and the Swiss and the Slovak rolled together in perfect Accord toward the Roubaix velodrome Dilli A's track cycling past couldn't carry its weight against the top speed of Peter Sagan who becomes the fifth rider to win Perry Roubaix while holding the title of world champion the list of victories in the Flanders is now complete for Peter Sagan at 28 and already winner of the Grand Prix III Gong bevel gam and the Tour of Flanders the Slovak has finally won Perry Roubaix almost audacious with ease the world champion even did his own repairs without doubt the strongest rider of this year's race Sagan steps a little further into the closed circle of great cycling champions I'm so tired after this race but I have to say this year I never been involving some crash I never feel tired and after to save energy and after I just did one one step forward like higher tack and I kept going until the finish and I've stay now much better than all year is what I finish very good obey I was a much more tired than today the intervals was sizable at the finish testament to the QuickTime recorded by Peter Sagan Silvan Delia in second and niki Terpstra in third completed the podium with four Belgians in the top ten as well as the American Taylor Finney who finished eighth so Perry Roubaix brings his Flanders classics to a close making way for the hilly Ardennes classics beginning with la flesche Vallon before the cycling monument of the edge Bastogne liege on Sunday the 22nd of April for now though we leave you with the most beautiful moments of this 116 edition of Perry Roubaix you
